data.description:
## Problem

In maestro session `term-1777515514586-a2eu8t`, OpenCode's first kno claim
exited 1, the agent stopped per its boundary rule, the take-loop fired the
"Your turn ended but beat is still in state X. Either complete the action
or run `kno rollback`" follow-up, and the agent picked rollback.
`kno rollback` correctly terminates the lease bound to the action step
(canonical kno behavior: rolling back action `implementation` released
lease `maestro-a85e` to `lease_terminated`). The take-loop did NOT
re-check lease state — it kept asking the agent to complete or rollback,
even though the lease the agent's prompt told it to use was now dead.
The agent then re-ran `kno claim ... --lease maestro-a85e` and got the
expected `error: external lease is in state 'lease_terminated' --
expected 'lease_ready'`. The user observed three rounds of that error
in the UI before noticing.

## Target state

Before sending any take-loop follow-up prompt to the agent, Foolery
re-fetches the bound lease (the one whose ID is embedded in the prompt
the agent received) and checks its state.

If the lease is in `lease_terminated` (or any non-active state):

1. The take-loop does NOT send a follow-up prompt.
2. A `FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE` red-banner is written to the session
   buffer (per the "Fail Loudly, Never Silently" rule in CLAUDE.md), with:
   - sessionId, beatId, currentBeatState, iteration, leaseId,
     leaseState, leaseTerminatedAt (if available), agentName,
     agentProvider, agentModel, agentVersion, currentStep, expectedStep,
     promptSource (which template built the dead prompt), follow-up
     count for this state.
3. The take-loop terminates the iteration cleanly and surfaces the
   condition to any subscribed session UI as a stderr banner event.
4. The lease audit emits `lease_dead_on_followup` (or similar) so the
   condition is grep-able after the fact.

## Acceptance

- The follow-up code path (search for `take_loop_follow_up_sent`)
  acquires lease state immediately before send and refuses to send if
  not in `lease_ready` (or whichever active states are valid).
- Banner uses the exact `FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE` marker phrase per
  CLAUDE.md so it is greppable.
- Hermetic unit test in `src/lib/__tests__/` injects a stub
  knots-backend that returns `lease_terminated` for the binding lease
  and asserts: zero follow-up sent, banner emitted, lease audit event
  emitted, take-loop iteration ends with the dispatch-failure outcome.
- maestro-ca91 reproduction (manual test): induce the same agent
  rollback path and verify the new behavior catches it on the very
  first follow-up evaluation, not the second or third.

## Why this matters

User feedback: "this is a clean and obvious bug. we need to inspect the
lease in each loop round/follow-up to ensure it is not terminated, since
we know that agents can roll back, which terminates the lease. if the
lease is in fact terminated, we log it with great detail."

This is a containment fix — it does not address why the agent rolled
back (knot dd9c covers the upstream cause investigation). It guarantees
that whenever an agent rolls back, the human sees a single loud failure
banner instead of the agent grinding on a dead lease forever.
